member.name: T:RateLimiting.Api.Configuration.InMemoryCustomerPolicyStore
member.summary:
In-memory implementation of .
Defines rate-limit policies per customer: • Customer A (Enterprise) — 1 000 req/s + 2 000 000 req/hour • Customer B (Standard) — 100 req/s + 200 000 req/hour • Default (Anonymous/IP) — 60 req/min
Multiple policies per customer use AND semantics: ALL must pass — the first failure short-circuits with 429.
Production note: Replace this class (or extend it) to load policies from a database, Redis, Azure App Configuration, or any config service. The rest of the pipeline never touches this class directly — it depends only on .

member.name: T:RateLimiting.Api.Configuration.OperationPolicyStore
member.summary:
Maps (HTTP method + path prefix) → a fine-grained rate-limit policy.
This is the third and most granular layer: Layer 1 — Customer (who is calling) Layer 2 — Service (which microservice) Layer 3 — Operation (which specific endpoint + verb) ← this class
Example: POST /payments/charge has a much tighter limit (10/min) than GET /payments/balance (60/min) even though both hit the Payment service.

member.name: M:RateLimiting.Api.Configuration.OperationPolicyStore.GetPolicy(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns the operation-level policy for the given HTTP method and path, or null if no specific operation policy is configured (service-level applies). Uses longest-prefix matching.

member.name: T:RateLimiting.Api.Controllers.DemoController
member.summary:
Demo endpoints for testing rate limiting behaviour.
Test with curl: # As Customer A (Enterprise — 1000/sec): curl -H "X-Customer-ID: customer-a" https://localhost:5001/api/demo/ping
# As Customer B (Standard — 100/sec): curl -H "X-Customer-ID: customer-b" https://localhost:5001/api/demo/ping
# Burst test (sends 200 requests rapidly): for i in {1..200}; do curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" \ -H "X-Customer-ID: customer-b" https://localhost:5001/api/demo/ping; done

member.name: M:RateLimiting.Api.Controllers.DemoController.Ping
member.summary: Simple health-check endpoint. The rate-limiting middleware evaluates before this action runs. On success: 200. If limited: 429 (never reaches this action).

member.name: M:RateLimiting.Api.Controllers.DemoController.GetPolicy(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the current rate-limit policy for a given customer. Useful for dashboards and debugging.

member.name: M:RateLimiting.Api.Controllers.DemoController.Slow(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Simulates a slow endpoint (100ms) to show that rate limiting acts before the endpoint executes.

member.name: T:RateLimiting.Api.Extensions.RateLimitingExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for registering rate-limiting services and middleware. Keeps Program.cs clean — all wiring in one place.

member.name: M:RateLimiting.Api.Extensions.RateLimitingExtensions.AddRateLimiting(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Registers all rate-limiting services into the DI container. Call this in Program.cs before builder.Build().

member.name: M:RateLimiting.Api.Extensions.RateLimitingExtensions.UseRateLimiting(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.IApplicationBuilder)
member.summary: Adds the to the request pipeline. Place this call BEFORE UseAuthentication and UseAuthorization so that 429 responses are returned cheaply without running auth logic.

member.name: T:RateLimiting.Api.Middleware.RateLimitMiddleware
member.summary:
Rate-Limiting Middleware ───────────────────────── Pipeline position: registered BEFORE authentication so 429 responses are returned cheaply, without executing token validation.
Per-request flow: 1. Resolve the caller's customer ID (header → JWT → API key → IP). 2. Load the customer's policy set from . 3. Evaluate each policy in order — ALL must pass (AND semantics). 4. Write RFC-compliant rate-limit headers on every response. 5. Return HTTP 429 with a JSON body if any policy fails.
